Qs.Headline & Outlet Metadata:
Fill only with externally verifiable facts:
• Headline text
• Outlet name
• Publication date (YYYY‑MM‑DD or "Unknown")
• Author name(s)
• Article section (Markets, Earnings, Opinion, etc.)
• Outlet ownership/funding (public company, nonprofit, etc.)
• Common media‑bias label if available (cite source)

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Bloomberg piece dated 2025‑05‑12 by Eric Martin, section ‘Economy’. Bloomberg is owned by Bloomberg L.P., generally rated ‘Center’ by Ad‑Fontes…”
Qs.Author/Outlet Background:
Summarize potential bias signals **not in the article text**:
• Author’s beat, past market calls, conflicts (e.g., ex‑employee of covered firm)
• Outlet’s revenue model (subscriptions, ads, sponsored content)
• Any historical retractions or pay‑for‑play allegations
If nothing noteworthy, write “No additional background found.”

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Reporter covers tech IPOs, once reprimanded for premature earnings leak. Outlet depends on terminal subscriptions…”
Qs.Key Financial Metrics Table:
Create a markdown table:
| # | Metric Quoted | Value & Period | Context Provided? (Y/N) | Standard Benchmark for Context |
Include earnings per share, revenue, GDP, CPI, unemployment, debt ratios—anything the article cites that underpins its thesis.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Metric 1: EPS Q1 2025 = $2.31, compared to $1.80 prior year → Context Y”
Qs.Data Source Audit:
List each numeric/data point with provenance in a markdown table:
| # | Data Point | Source (SEC filing, BLS release, company PR, proprietary model…) | First‑hand? (Original/Secondary) | Reliability (1‑5) |
Score reliability like fact‑checkers (5 = audited SEC report, 1 = anonymous blog).

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Revenue figure from 10‑Q → Original → 5. Inflation stat from commentator’s Substack → Secondary → 2.”
Qs.Analyst Commentary Bias Check:
Bullet‑list analyst/expert quotes and note any potential bias:
• Employment (sell‑side bank, independent research, activist short‑seller)
• Disclosed holdings/ratings (Buy, Hold, Sell)
• Track‑record accuracy if known

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “JPM analyst rates ‘Overweight’—works for underwriter of company’s bond issue...”
Qs.Macro Context & Historical Comparison:
Provide 3–5 sentences situating the article’s claims within broader economic trends:
• Compare cited metrics to 1‑, 5‑, or 10‑year averages
• Note business cycle phase, policy changes, or shocks (e.g., rate hikes)
• Flag if article cherry‑picks an unusually favorable baseline.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “EPS growth appears large but is below 5‑yr CAGR; publication omits pandemic low base‑effect…”
Qs.Forward‑Looking Language Assessment:
Identify forward‑looking or speculative statements. Present in a markdown table:
| # | Statement (paraphrase) | Modal Verbs/Adverbs Used | Evidence Offered? (Y/N) | Speculative Level (Low/Med/High) |
Include CEO forecasts, analyst price targets, macro predictions.

*Sample chain‑of‑thought:* “Claim: ‘Shares could surge 30% next quarter’ → uses ‘could’ → Evidence N → Speculative High.”
